So what happens is after your 3000 times saying something, you lose the certainty that you want something not inside, but you become like habituated and you kind of tone down your enthusiasm because you start feeling so I keep saying the same words. How can I sound as excited? You have to. You cannot afford to lose that enthusiasm than exciting that certainty because they're hearing it for the first time. We must get the prospect to this ultra high level of logical certainty for the first 10. Have to. Everything else depends on that because of this idea of positive intent. People don't buy things that they think will make their life worse. They just don't. They buy things that will make their life better. They think they'll make their life better. That doesn't always end up being the case, but we act from positive intent. You must take the time to make an airtight logical case and don't blow past it if you hadn't.
